title: Reserve and Auto-Release Blood Units Based on Pending Requests
description: The system shall reserve specific blood units for pending requests and automatically release them after a defined period or when no longer needed.
rationale: To ensure efficient allocation of blood units and prevent waste due to delayed processing of requests.
business_value: Improves operational efficiency by reducing the time from request to availability of blood units, thereby ensuring timely treatment for patients.

source.description: As a blood bank manager I need to reserve specific blood units for pending requests and automatically release them after a defined period or when no longer needed So that blood units are efficiently allocated and not wasted

acceptance_criteria:
Given a pending request for a specific blood type and components
When the system reserves the matching blood units
Then those units are marked as reserved and unavailable for other requests

Given a reservation timeout period is configured
When the timeout expires for a reserved unit
Then that unit is automatically released and becomes available

Given a pending request is cancelled
When the cancellation is processed
Then all units reserved for that request are released
preconditions: A pending request exists with a specified blood type and component requirements
The system has access to the necessary inventory of blood units
The reservation timeout period is configured in the system settings
postconditions: All reserved blood units are marked as unavailable for other requests
Unused or expired reserved blood units are automatically released and made available again
All units previously reserved for a cancelled request are returned to the inventory pool
functional_constraints: The system must accurately track and manage the reservation status of each blood unit
The automatic release mechanism must respect both timeout expiration and cancellation events
The system must handle multiple simultaneous reservations and ensure no conflicts arise

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system must process reservation requests within a reasonable time frame to avoid bottlenecks
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available with at least 99.9% uptime during peak blood request periods
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must be able to scale up to accommodate an increase in the number of pending requests and inventory units without significant performance degradation
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and actionable, and the user interface should provide visual feedback on reservation statuses

edge_cases: What happens when a reservation timeout period is set to zero?
How should the system handle simultaneous requests for the same blood type and components?
What if the inventory does not have enough units to meet multiple concurrent reservations?
dependencies: User Interface (UI) for Blood Bank Managers

interfaces.name: Blood Inventory Database
interfaces.description: The system interacts with this database to retrieve and update blood unit information, reservation statuses, and inventory levels.

interfaces.name: Inventory Management API
interfaces.description: This API is used to communicate with the Blood Inventory Management System to check availability and update inventory after a reservation or release.

interfaces.name: Blood Bank Manager Interface
interfaces.description: The system interfaces with this UI to allow blood bank managers to view pending requests, manage reservations, and receive notifications about the status of their reservations.

test_cases.description: Reserve a single blood unit for a pending request with a timeout period.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should mark the reserved unit as unavailable and set a timer based on the configured timeout period.

test_cases.description: Release a reserved unit after the reservation timeout expires.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should automatically release the reserved unit and make it available for other requests.

test_cases.description: Cancel a pending request and release all associated reserved units.
test_cases.expected_result: All units previously reserved for the cancelled request should be returned to the inventory pool and marked as available.
